cityDNA.coreFantasy: A kobold industrial hive where small citizens maintain oversized forges, black markets, command systems, alchemical labs, and scrap warrens under volcanic pressure.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Embervein is not a primitive cave warren or comic kobold clutter; it is an old technical civilization built from risk, heat, coordination, and scale mismatch.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Embervein turns small-body survival, cavern heat, forge labor, hidden trade, command discipline, alchemical invention, and lower-warren salvage into an industrial civic hive.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Forge towers, smoke stacks, basalt halls, obsidian keeps, molten cracks, blue alchemical lamps, black-market arcades, command standards, scaffold bridges, crawl routes, formula archives, scrap markets, and patched tenements.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A kobold civilization of quick technical workers, clan maintenance crews, cautious inventors, market runners, command subjects, alchemy assistants, and lower-warren salvage families.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Crucibles, forge beacons, smoke stacks, molten cracks, blue lamps, vault doors, command standards, crawl routes, and scrap markets symbolize ingenuity under pressure.

canonLocks: Embervein is a mature kobold subterranean-industrial city, not a primitive cave warren.
The Wonder is the Kobold Ember-Warren Crucible Hive.
The Beacon is the Great Forge Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Molten Crack Glow Iron Mandate Keep.
The five primary districts are Heavy Industry / Foundry, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Military Command, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, and Slums / Informal Settlement.

The uploaded district anchors include Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, Military Command Hall, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, and Patched Tenement Nook.
provisionalCanon: Embervein's highest civic virtue is ingenuity under pressure.
Its central tension is invention and production versus workers being treated as replaceable machine parts.
The hidden economy is not marginal; it is a tolerated pressure valve that keeps official industry functioning.
rejectedReads: Embervein is a primitive cave warren.
The forge is the only important civic system.
The slums and black market are incidental rather than structurally central.
cautionFlags: Use kobold scale constantly: low doors, tight balconies, crawl routes, scaffold platforms, and small workers under huge machines.
Keep industry and poverty in the same visual system.
Do not let forge imagery erase black-market, command, alchemy, or slum systems.
Preserve exact sidecar anchors instead of smoothing them into generic forge or slum labels.

environment.environmentalLogic: Embervein treats cavern heat, lava glow, smoke, low passages, and underground density as civic infrastructure. Survival depends on converting pressure into production, invention, command, and salvage.

skyline: Kobold Ember-Warren Crucible Hive as the glowing wonder-scale cavern heart
Molten Crack Glow Iron Mandate Keep as central authority
Great Forge Beacon as industrial signal heart
command towers, blue-lit labs, smoke stacks, black-market tunnels, and scrap warrens stacked around cavern walls

civilization.people: Citizens are kobolds: small, quick, technical, nervous-smart, clan/warren-aware, excellent at maintenance, and used to living under structures much larger than themselves.
civilization.governance: Authority is forge-mandate and command-industrial: power flows from production, military discipline, alchemical control, tolerated shadow trade, and the Keep that governs the warren.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is secondary to work rites, forge rituals, clan obligations, invention taboos, and fear-respect for the Crucible Hive.
customs: checking vents and seals before work
using crawl routes by rank and trust
treating small maintenance errors as civic emergencies

streets.appearance: Cramped, vertical, hot, and mechanical: scaffold bridges, blue alchemical lamps, orange forge glow, smoke stacks, market tunnels, command stairs, standard towers, and lower scrap warrens crowd cavern walls.
streets.movement: Movement is small-body and route-dense: crawl passages, low balconies, scaffold platforms, command stairs, market tunnels, forge lanes, and maintenance routes.
streets.lighting: Orange forge light and blue alchemical lamps compete with black-market lanterns and molten cracks; shadows are deep under machines.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather is internal: heat shifts, smoke pressure, vent failures, steam bursts, and condensation change movement and mood.
streets.dayCycle: Work cycles follow forge shifts, command musters, lab access, market openings, and scrap collection.
streets.nightCycle: Night favors black-market tunnels, lower-warren repair, reduced forge glow, and blue lab light against cavern dark.

housing.wealthy: Command- or forge-adjacent compact residences with private scaffold access, blue lamps, sealed vents, and protected storage.
housing.middle: Clan workshops, low-door apartments, gearwright rooms, shared balconies, and tool-lined walls near work routes.
housing.poor: Lower scrap warrens, patched tenements, tight tunnels, shared heat, salvage storage, and cramped family alcoves beneath larger systems.

districts.name: Heavy Industry / Foundry
districts.civicPurpose: Production, industrial alarm, labor coordination, forge identity, and citywide heat management.
districts.visualIdentity: Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge Beacon, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook, forge floors, chains, vent shafts, orange heat, and workers under huge machines.
narrativeUse: tiny maintenance failure with huge consequences

districts.name: Underworld / Hidden Economy
districts.civicPurpose: Shadow commerce, debt, contraband, informal supply, and tolerated survival networks.
districts.visualIdentity: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, coded stalls, low tunnels, and guarded doors.

districts.name: Military Command
districts.civicPurpose: Rank, musters, defensive discipline, command protection, and official force inside the hive.
districts.visualIdentity: Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook, command stairs, military platforms, and small guards under large standards.

districts.name: Arcane / Alchemical Labs
districts.civicPurpose: Dangerous research, formula control, reagent management, and magical-industrial invention.
districts.visualIdentity: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, glass vials, sealed doors, and blue light.

districts.name: Slums / Informal Settlement
districts.civicPurpose: Lower-warren survival, repair culture, salvage economy, and pressure beneath official systems.
districts.visualIdentity: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, salvage piles, cramped passages, and low lamps.

wonder.name: Kobold Ember-Warren Crucible Hive
wonder.symbolicRole: Kobold warren identity, forge heat, hive coordination, and citywide pressure made into one public engine.
wonder.visualRole: Huge glowing crucible-hive structure embedded in cavern walls with forge tiers, warrens, scaffolds, and industrial routes.
wonder.civicRole: Primary engine-symbol of production, invention, mass labor, maintenance, and catastrophic pressure.
storyHooks: A tiny maintenance failure in the Hive threatens a city-scale system.
The Hive proves the city needs the people it tries to treat as parts.

beacon.name: Great Forge Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Industrial output and emergency made public signal.
beacon.visualRole: Signal landmark rising from the foundry amid smoke stacks, forge halls, molten glow, and blue-orange light.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates forge crews, black-market runners, command officers, alchemists, and scrap families.
storyHooks: A Beacon shift changes every work route in the warren.

seatOfPower.name: Molten Crack Glow Iron Mandate Keep
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Production quotas, command law, and molten legitimacy made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Iron keep lit by molten cracks, command geometry, black stone, mandate halls, sealed archives, and high platforms.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Rulings about labor, inventions, military output, accidents, and black-market tolerance.
storyHooks: The Keep must decide whether a useful black-market invention becomes legal.

majorLandmarks.name: Black Market Arcade
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: The shadow supply line that keeps the hive alive.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Arcade of coded stalls, vault doors, mist, blue-black lanterns, and guarded passages.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Informal economy, debt, contraband, and supply workaround.
storyHooks: A broker knows which official machine is about to fail.

majorLandmarks.name: Command Bastion
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Small bodies organized beneath enormous standards.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Command platform with banners, standard tower, stairs, guard posts, and military geometry.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Musters, orders, defense coordination, and official force.
storyHooks: A command order makes the repair crews choose between safety and obedience.

majorLandmarks.name: Alchemy Lab
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Blue-lit lab with glass vessels, formula archive, sealed shelves, and reagent light.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Formula control, reagent work, invention, and magical-industrial risk management.
storyHooks: A formula archive proves the black market copied official research.

majorLandmarks.name: Scrap Market
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Survival through salvage beneath official production.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Lower-warren salvage market with lantern monument, patched tenements, repair stalls, and scrap piles.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Repair culture, poverty survival, informal supply, and overlooked evidence.
storyHooks: The scrap market has already fixed the problem the Keep denies exists.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge Beacon, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook, forge workers, chains, vent shafts, and oversized machinery scaled against small kobolds.
S2 must show Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, coded stalls, brokers, and low tunnels.
S3 must show Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook, command stairs, guards, and large standards over small figures.
S4 must show Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, vials, reagents, sealed shelves, and blue light.
S5 must show Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, salvage piles, patched cloth, and lower-warren families.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show a subterranean industrial cavern city with forge-orange glow, blue alchemical lamps, smoke stacks, molten cracks, scaffold bridges, warren tunnels, command towers, black-market vaults, and scrap warrens.

The Kobold Ember-Warren Crucible Hive should dominate as the wonder-scale cavern engine; the Great Forge Beacon should anchor the foundry; the Molten Crack Glow Iron Mandate Keep should appear as central authority.
Use scale mismatch: huge machines and halls must make kobolds look ingenious, vulnerable, and overworked rather than silly.
requiredVisualElements: Molten Crack Glow Iron Mandate Keep
elementsToAvoid: forge imagery that erases black-market or slum systems
